query:
INSERT INTO knowledge_base (
  compound_name,
  category,
  summary,
  mechanisms,
  clinical_evidence,
  dosing,
  contraindications,
  interactions,
  sources,
  tags,
  created_by
) VALUES (
  'Statins (HMG-CoA Reductase Inhibitors)',
  'Lipid-Lowering Agent / Anti-Inflammatory / Vascular Protective',
  'Statins are widely prescribed cholesterol-lowering drugs whose clinical benefits may derive more from pleiotropic effects (anti-inflammatory, vascular protective) than from LDL-C reduction alone. Meta-analyses show weak correlation between magnitude of LDL-C lowering and cardiovascular outcomes, suggesting the "LDL-C hypothesis" is an invalid surrogate marker. Benefits are most pronounced in metabolically unhealthy populations (obesity, diabetes, inflammation) where pleiotropic effects address underlying pathology.',
  JSON_ARRAY(
    JSON_OBJECT('name', 'HMG-CoA Reductase Inhibition (Primary Mechanism)', 'description', 'Statins inhibit HMG-CoA reductase, the rate-limiting enzyme in hepatic cholesterol synthesis. This reduces intracellular cholesterol, upregulates LDL receptors, and lowers circulating LDL-C and ApoB. While this is the intended mechanism, clinical evidence suggests LDL-C reduction alone does not fully explain cardiovascular benefits.'),
    JSON_OBJECT('name', 'Anti-Inflammatory Effects (Pleiotropic)', 'description', 'Statins reduce systemic inflammation independent of cholesterol lowering. They decrease C-reactive protein (CRP), interleukin-6 (IL-6), and tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-α). This anti-inflammatory action is particularly beneficial in metabolically unhealthy patients with chronic low-grade inflammation driving atherosclerosis.'),
    JSON_OBJECT('name', 'Vascular Function Improvement (Pleiotropic)', 'description', 'Statins improve endothelial function by increasing nitric oxide (NO) bioavailability, promoting vasodilation and improving blood flow. They stabilize atherosclerotic plaques, reduce oxidative stress, and enhance vascular repair mechanisms. These effects contribute to cardiovascular protection beyond lipid modification.'),
    JSON_OBJECT('name', 'Antithrombotic Effects (Pleiotropic)', 'description', 'Statins reduce platelet aggregation and thrombus formation, decreasing risk of acute coronary events. They modulate coagulation pathways and fibrinolysis, providing additional cardiovascular protection independent of cholesterol levels.')
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Weak correlation (r² near zero) between LDL-C reduction magnitude and cardiovascular event reduction across 20 RCTs with ~200,000 patients', 'source', 'European Heart Journal Umbrella Review'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Very little, if any, association between LDL-C reduction and improvements in death or heart attacks across 21 RCTs', 'source', 'JAMA Internal Medicine 2022'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Cardiovascular benefit proportional to LDL-C reduction in high-risk, secondary prevention populations (conflicting evidence)', 'source', 'JAMA 2016 (Silverman et al.)'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Greatest benefit observed in metabolically unhealthy patients (obesity, high triglycerides, low HDL, type 2 diabetes) where pleiotropic effects address underlying pathology', 'source', 'Meta-analysis synthesis'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Minimal to no impact on outcomes in otherwise healthy individuals with elevated LDL-C as sole risk factor', 'source', 'Primary prevention trial analyses'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'LDL-C is an "inappropriate surrogate marker" - changing the number does not guarantee better clinical outcomes', 'source', 'Critical appraisal of surrogate endpoints')
  ),
  JSON_OBJECT(
    'typical', 'Varies by statin: Atorvastatin 10-80 mg/day, Rosuvastatin 5-40 mg/day, Simvastatin 10-40 mg/day',
    'range', 'Low-intensity (LDL-C ↓<30%), Moderate-intensity (LDL-C ↓30-50%), High-intensity (LDL-C ↓≥50%)',
    'notes', 'Dosing should be individualized based on patient risk profile, metabolic health status, and treatment goals. Evidence suggests metabolically unhealthy patients benefit most from pleiotropic effects regardless of baseline LDL-C.'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Active liver disease or unexplained persistent elevations in serum transaminases',
    'Pregnancy and lactation (teratogenic risk)',
    'Known hypersensitivity to statins',
    'Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors with certain statins (atorvastatin, simvastatin, lovastatin)',
    'History of statin-induced myopathy or rhabdomyolysis'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Fibrates: Increased risk of myopathy; use with caution, especially gemfibrozil',
    'CYP3A4 inhibitors (e.g., clarithromycin, itraconazole, grapefruit juice): Increase statin levels; dose adjustment or alternative statin needed',
    'Warfarin: Statins may potentiate anticoagulant effect; monitor INR',
    'Cyclosporine: Significantly increases statin exposure; dose reduction required',
    'Niacin: May increase myopathy risk when combined with statins'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'https://academic.oup.com/eurheartj/ (European Heart Journal Umbrella Review)',
    'https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamainternalmedicine (JAMA Internal Medicine 2022 meta-analysis)',
    'https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jama (JAMA 2016 Silverman et al.)',
    'https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/ (Pleiotropic effects research)',
    'https://www.ahajournals.org/ (American Heart Association statin guidelines)'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Cholesterol Lowering',
    'Anti-inflammatory',
    'Vascular Protection',
    'Cardiovascular Disease',
    'Pleiotropic Effects',
    'Metabolic Syndrome',
    'Secondary Prevention',
    'LDL-C Surrogate Marker',
    'Endothelial Function',
    'Patient Selection',
    'Evidence-Based Medicine',
    'Surrogate Endpoint Critique'
  ),
  1
);
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 34DkbYAaF6MBWwK.96fd8e3f3a28 --database 6XDPcfqtDX3GCRKvFSNyjg --execute INSERT INTO knowledge_base (

    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Very little, if any, association between LDL-C reduction and improvements in death or heart attacks across 21 RCTs', 'source', 'JAMA Internal Medicine 2022'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Cardiovascular benefit proportional to LDL-C reduction in high-risk, secondary prevention populations (conflicting evidence)', 'source', 'JAMA 2016 (Silverman et al.)'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Greatest benefit observed in metabolically unhealthy patients (obesity, high triglycerides, low HDL, type 2 diabetes) where pleiotropic effects address underlying pathology', 'source', 'Meta-analysis synthesis'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'Minimal to no impact on outcomes in otherwise healthy individuals with elevated LDL-C as sole risk factor', 'source', 'Primary prevention trial analyses'),
    JSON_OBJECT('finding', 'LDL-C is an "inappropriate surrogate marker" - changing the number does not guarantee better clinical outcomes', 'source', 'Critical appraisal of surrogate endpoints')

  JSON_OBJECT(
    'typical', 'Varies by statin: Atorvastatin 10-80 mg/day, Rosuvastatin 5-40 mg/day, Simvastatin 10-40 mg/day',
    'range', 'Low-intensity (LDL-C ↓<30%), Moderate-intensity (LDL-C ↓30-50%), High-intensity (LDL-C ↓≥50%)',
    'notes', 'Dosing should be individualized based on patient risk profile, metabolic health status, and treatment goals. Evidence suggests metabolically unhealthy patients benefit most from pleiotropic effects regardless of baseline LDL-C.'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Active liver disease or unexplained persistent elevations in serum transaminases',
    'Pregnancy and lactation (teratogenic risk)',
    'Known hypersensitivity to statins',
    'Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors with certain statins (atorvastatin, simvastatin, lovastatin)',
    'History of statin-induced myopathy or rhabdomyolysis'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Fibrates: Increased risk of myopathy; use with caution, especially gemfibrozil',
    'CYP3A4 inhibitors (e.g., clarithromycin, itraconazole, grapefruit juice): Increase statin levels; dose adjustment or alternative statin needed',
    'Warfarin: Statins may potentiate anticoagulant effect; monitor INR',
    'Cyclosporine: Significantly increases statin exposure; dose reduction required',
    'Niacin: May increase myopathy risk when combined with statins'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'https://academic.oup.com/eurheartj/ (European Heart Journal Umbrella Review)',
    'https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamainternalmedicine (JAMA Internal Medicine 2022 meta-analysis)',
    'https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jama (JAMA 2016 Silverman et al.)',
    'https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/ (Pleiotropic effects research)',
    'https://www.ahajournals.org/ (American Heart Association statin guidelines)'
  ),
  JSON_ARRAY(
    'Cholesterol Lowering',
    'Anti-inflammatory',
    'Vascular Protection',
    'Cardiovascular Disease',
    'Pleiotropic Effects',
    'Metabolic Syndrome',
    'Secondary Prevention',
    'LDL-C Surrogate Marker',
    'Endothelial Function',
    'Patient Selection',
    'Evidence-Based Medicine',
    'Surrogate Endpoint Critique'
  ),
  1
);
